Off Calendar Jan-27-2020 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Mar-17-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff'S Motion To Reopen Discovery For Expert Disclosure Only, Or, In The Alternative, Determine That No Expert Disclosure Exchange Date Was Confirmed In Writing (Per Ex Parte Order Filed 1/10/20)
Off Calendar Jan-22-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Joseph Bravo of The Bravo Law Offices 415-512-6700 CourtCall appearance for plaintiff. / Atty Anderson Franco of De La Pena & Holiday LLP 415-268-8000 appeared for defendant.

Argued - Plaintiff'S Motion To Reopen Discovery For Expert Disclosure Only, Or, In The Alternative, Determine That No Expert Disclosure Exchange Date Was Confirmed In Writing.

The Motion is denied. Code of Civil Procedure section 2034.720 governs the outcome. While a reasonable argument can be made that Plaintiff failed to submit expert designations as a result of mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect, she failed, after learning of the missed deadline, to promptly serve expert witness information. Cottini v. Enloe Medical Center (2014) 226 Ca1.App.4th 401, 421. That is a mandatory requirement compelling denial of this Motion.
There are mitigating factors supporting a reduction in the amount of sanctions awarded. First, Mr. Bravo specifically asked that all emails be copied to his associate. While that request was usually complied with, it did not happen with the email confirming the new date for exchange of expert information. Second, the minute order on the Motion to Continue Trial states: "The court denied the request to extend discovery deadlines without prejudice, to be brought before the discovery department 302." Thus, Plaintiff was specifically authorized to bring the instant Motion. The handwritten order by Judge Wong is silent on this point. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $1,700. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D32/JPT)
